author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
hari hari! kabe mora hoibe sudine
kelikautuka raṅge kariba sevane

lalitā⋅viśākhā sane, jateka saṅgīra gaṇe,
maṇḍalī kariba doha meli’
rāi kānu⋅kare dhari’, nṛtya kare phiri’ phiri’,
nirakhi’ goṅā’ba kutūhalī

alasa⋅viśrāma⋅ghare, govardhana⋅girivare,
rāikānu karibe śayana
narottama⋅dāsa kaya, ei jena mora haya,
anukṣaṇa caraṇa sevana
en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will I have that auspicious day when I will perform service in the playful pastimes?

With Lalitā and Viśākhā, and all the group of companions, I will make a circle, bringing the Two together. Holding the hands of Rāi and Kānu, They will dance round and round; I will pass the time watching with curiosity.

In the house of leisurely rest, on the best of hills, Govardhana, Rāi and Kānu will lie down. Narottama Dāsa says: May this be mine—service to the feet at every moment.
